# STS2 Trace Schema

Envelope schema id: `sts2.envelope/1` (SPEC §8.1). Journal lines are single-line JSON with this field order:

| Field | Type | Meaning |
|---|---|---|
| schema | string | envelope schema identifier |
| runId | string | stable per process run |
| seq | number | gapless monotonic, assigned by the coordinator (I5) |
| ts | string | UTC round-trip (Z); replay uses recorded values |
| kind | string | one of the kinds below |
| sessionId | string? | logical connection/session id |
| corr | string? | JSON-RPC id, effect id, or correlation id |
| cause | number? | seq of the producing envelope; null only for external inbound and root control |
| type | string | method, command, event, effect, metric, or diagnostic name |
| configVersion | number | config snapshot version |
| digest | string | sha256 of the canonical payload, present even when elided |
| payload | any? | inline payload when capture permits |
| payloadMeta | any? | redaction/elision metadata |

## Kinds

- `cmd`
- `config.changed`
- `control`
- `diag`
- `effect.req`
- `effect.res`
- `evt`
- `metric`
- `rpc.in.notify`
- `rpc.in.request`
- `rpc.out.error`
- `rpc.out.notify`
- `rpc.out.result`
- `state.snapshot`
- `timer.due`

## Redaction markers

Redacted scalar: `{"$redacted":true,"kind":"sql","digest":"sha256:...","bytes":N}` — the wrapper carries the original scalar's authoritative digest (SPEC §8.2).
Secret token: `secret:sha256:<12-hex>:<counter>` — real material lives only in the in-memory side table (SPEC §8.5).
Canonical payload form: UTF-8, ordinal-sorted keys, no insignificant whitespace, number tokens verbatim (D-0007).
